entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding. Let me create an updated plan based on what's already implemented:

Plan: Multi-Instance Qt IDE Enhancement Review
Your RawrXD IDE already has comprehensive multi-instance support with terminal integration and multi-threading. The system is configured with:

100 max AI chat panels (ai_chat_panel_manager.hpp)
Session persistence with RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
Agent orchestration with multi-agent coordination
Terminal manager with PowerShell/CMD support
Resource management and health monitoring
The CLI also has multi-instance support with unique PIDs and dynamic port allocation. Let me clarify what's implemented vs what might need enhancement.

Steps
Verify multi-instance IDE launch safety - Check if src/qtapp/main_qt.cpp and src/ide_main_window.cpp have instance locking, confirm the 100-panel limit in AIChatPanelManager works across multiple IDE instances, test if multiple IDE windows can safely share the same port or use different ports, and verify session persistence doesn't conflict between instances

Review terminal integration threading - Examine src/qtapp/TerminalManager.cpp and src/qtapp/TerminalWidget.cpp for async PowerShell/CMD execution, check if terminal operations use QThreadPool or QtConcurrent, verify real_time_terminal_pool.cpp integration status, and confirm terminal sessions persist across IDE restarts via session_persistence.cpp

Document existing agent/sub-agent architecture - Map out the agent hierarchy in AgentOrchestrator.h showing how 20+ agents coordinate, explain AgentChatPane[ relationship to the 100-panel ](http://_vscodecontentref_/3)AIChatPanelManager[ limit, clarify if "20 windows" refers to agent panes or full IDE instances, and document how sub-agents spawn and communicate via ](http://_vscodecontentref_/4)AgentMessage system

Test CLI multi-instance behavior - Confirm rawrxd_cli.cpp unique port allocation (11434 + PID mod 100) works reliably, verify multiple CLI instances don't conflict on settings files, test shell command execution (PowerShell/CMD) doesn't block other instances, and check if CLI can communicate with Qt IDE instances via IPC

Enhance documentation - Create user guide explaining: how to safely launch multiple IDE instances, the 100 chat panel limit per instance, how agent orchestration distributes work across panels, PowerShell/CMD terminal usage patterns, and session persistence/recovery workflow

Further Considerations
Clarify "20 windows" specification - Does this mean 20 AgentChatPane widgets active simultaneously (currently limited to 100), or 20 full IDE application instances running? The current m_maxPanels = 100 suggests the latter

Instance coordination strategy - Should multiple IDE instances share a central agent orchestrator, or remain isolated? Consider implementing IPC via QLocalServer for cross-instance agent collaboration if needed

Resource enforcement beyond panels - The AIChatPanelManager limits panels to 100, but are there limits on: terminal sessions per instance, active agents in AgentOrchestrator, concurrent model inference requests, or session persistence storage size?'
